Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Maze puzzle with a white-cross player inside a gray structure. Charcoal (c) cells form corridors. Exit is orange+blue blocks at bottom-right of the structure. Player needs to navigate through corridors to reach the exit.
- Goal model: Move the exit block to overlap with the gap corridor or the player. Testing gap alignment first (fewer actions).
- Action model: UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT move the player through the maze.
- Plan: Move exit to gap area with 2 UP + 2 LEFT to get exit to rows 30-34, cols 30-34 (overlapping gap at rows 30-35, cols 29-33).

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The exit moved up into the corridor. Let me check its new position and see if it can continue moving up toward the blue item.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check exit position
curr_ascii = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

print("Corridor area (rows 15-30, cols 30-42):")
for r in range(15, 31):
    print(f"  {r}: {curr_ascii[r][30:42]}")

# Find exit position
exit_rows = []
for r in range(len(curr_ascii)):
    for c in range(len(curr_ascii[r])):
        if curr_ascii[r][c] == 'O':
            exit_rows.append(r)

print(f"\nExit orange rows: {min(exit_rows)}-{max(exit_rows)}")

# Check if exit can continue up
print("\nBlue item (rows 10-14, cols 33-40):")
for r in range(10, 15):
    print(f"  {r}: {curr_ascii[r][33:40]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
